filter_pushdown.note: days_since_epoch: 1994-01-01=8035, 1995-01-01=10592. Table is sorted by l_shipdate — zone map skip rate is very high (most of 600 zones fall entirely before 8035 or after 10592).
Scaled by 100. Applied as secondary zone map prune on idx_lineitem_discount_qty.
Scaled by 100. Tertiary zone map prune: skip zones where min_quantity >= 2400.

predicate_order: l_shipdate (most selective, sorted column, zone-map kills ~86% zones)
logical_plan.output: SUM(l_extendedprice * l_discount) as int64_t, divide by scale_factor^2 (10000) at output

data_structures.note: No GROUP BY — each thread holds a single int64_t partial_sum. Final reduce is 64 additions. No hash table needed.

zone_map_usage.layout: uint32_t num_zones, then per zone: [min:int32_t(4B), max:int32_t(4B), count:uint32_t(4B)] = 12B/zone, 600 zones
zone_map_usage.skip_condition: skip zone if zone.max < 8035 OR zone.min >= 10592
zone_map_usage.purpose: Primary block pruning — since lineitem is sorted by l_shipdate, expected ~85-90% of 600 zones skipped

zone_map_usage.layout: uint32_t num_zones, then per zone: [min:int64_t(8B), max:int64_t(8B), count:uint32_t(4B)] = 20B/zone, 600 zones
zone_map_usage.skip_condition_discount: skip zone if zone.max_discount < 500 OR zone.min_discount > 700
zone_map_usage.purpose: Secondary block pruning on surviving shipdate zones — further reduces scan work

scan_detail.phase_1: Load idx_lineitem_shipdate zone map. Mark each of 600 zones as alive/dead based on shipdate range [8035, 10592). Expected ~60-90 survivor zones covering ~6M-9M rows.
scan_detail.phase_2: For each alive shipdate zone, cross-check idx_lineitem_discount_qty zone map. Skip zones where discount or quantity zones are fully out of predicate range. Further prunes survivor set.
scan_detail.phase_3: Parallel morsel scan over surviving zones only. Each thread: read l_shipdate, l_discount, l_quantity, l_extendedprice for its morsels. Apply per-row predicates, accumulate revenue.
scan_detail.fused_loop: Single pass: read 4 columns together per row. Evaluate l_shipdate first (short-circuit), then l_discount, then l_quantity. If all pass: partial_sum += l_extendedprice * l_discount.

parallelism.note: 64 cores available. Morsel size ~65K rows targets L3 cache (44MB / 64 threads ≈ 687KB/thread; 65K rows × 4 columns × 8B = ~2MB — fits well given sequential access). Threads pull surviving zone ranges from a shared atomic counter.
parallelism.work_distribution: Each thread claims a zone-block range via atomic fetch_add. Threads processing boundary zones do per-row predicate checks; interior zones (if entire zone is in shipdate range) still do per-row discount/quantity checks.
parallelism.load_balance: Natural — surviving zones are concentrated in ~1994 data band; all threads share this range uniformly.

simd_opportunity.detail: Process 8×int32 shipdate values per instruction (_mm256_cmpgt_epi32). Process 4×int64 discount/quantity values per instruction. Use branchless mask accumulation. Compiler auto-vectorization with #pragma omp simd + __restrict__ is acceptable fallback.
simd_opportunity.benefit: 8x throughput on shipdate filter, 4x on discount/quantity int64 comparisons

physical_plan.memory_detail: mmap all four column files (l_shipdate, l_discount, l_quantity, l_extendedprice). Access is sequential within surviving zones. OS prefetcher handles readahead effectively. No random access.

output_encoding.revenue_internal: int64_t sum of (l_extendedprice * l_discount), both already scaled by 100
output_encoding.revenue_final: divide by 10000 (scale_factor^2) to get decimal result, or emit as scaled int64 per output spec

estimated_cost.bottleneck: HDD sequential read bandwidth for surviving zone column data. With 64 threads and mmap, effective bandwidth should be near memory bandwidth (~50 GB/s), not HDD limit, since data is likely cached after first access.
notes: Q6 is pure scan+filter+scalar-agg — no joins, no hash tables, no GROUP BY. Complexity is entirely I/O + predicate evaluation.
Zone map pruning on sorted l_shipdate is the critical optimization: skips ~85%+ of blocks before any data is read.
Secondary zone map on discount/qty provides additional 20-40% prune on survivors.
64-thread parallelism with thread-local accumulators eliminates all synchronization during hot loop.
AVX2 SIMD on predicate columns gives ~4-8x instruction throughput improvement.
Result: with pruning + parallelism + SIMD, target <15ms to match Umbra.
